flagship.name: WhatsApp AI Agent — Multi-Tenant SaaS
flagship.description: Multi-tenant WhatsApp Business AI SaaS: an admin dashboard lets each business workspace configure an LLM-powered agent that handles customer conversations over the real WhatsApp Business Cloud API, with automated drip-sequence follow-ups and an appointment/reservation booking state machine.
highlights: Row-Level-Security-based tenant isolation across contacts, conversations, and knowledge-base tables, backed by integration tests (found and fixed a real cross-tenant data-leak bug via a live RLS audit)
Booking state machine handling multi-step reservation/appointment flows over chat
Drip sequence engine for scheduled automated follow-up messages
Groq-backed LLM tool-calling agent with per-workspace knowledge-base injection
Real Vitest integration test suite, including tests that assert against the live Supabase project
bullets: Built a multi-tenant WhatsApp Business AI SaaS (Next.js 16, Supabase, Groq) letting businesses deploy an LLM agent over their real WhatsApp number, with per-tenant RLS-enforced data isolation
Designed a booking state machine and drip-sequence engine for automated appointment scheduling and follow-up messaging
Wrote integration tests that queried the live database directly to verify tenant isolation, catching and fixing a real cross-tenant RLS misconfiguration

flagship.name: Job Search Automation Platform (this app)
flagship.description: Self-built, fully deployed multi-agent job-search system: scrapes 12 job sources concurrently, scores every listing against a candidate profile via LLM + keyword heuristics, generates tailored ATS-friendly PDF CVs and cover letters per job, tracks the full application pipeline, and includes an AI-driven mock interview trainer with scored feedback. FastAPI backend on Render, Next.js frontend on Vercel, shared Supabase Postgres database.
highlights: 12-source job scraper (Internshala, LinkedIn, WeWorkRemotely, Remotive, RemoteOK, Jobicy, Arbeitnow, Remote.co, TheMuse, Himalayas, HN "Who is Hiring", Adzuna) unified into one pipeline
Two-stage scoring: cheap keyword heuristic first, LLM batch-scoring only for uncertain (35-65) matches — keeps API usage low
Per-job CV/cover-letter tailoring: relevance-ranks the candidate's own project portfolio by keyword overlap with the job description before generating
Server-Sent-Events streaming for live scrape progress in the UI
Pluggable AI provider layer: Gemini for scoring/production, an optional local Claude Code CLI path for higher-quality CV writing during development
Deployed as a real hybrid architecture (Render + Vercel + Supabase), not just a local script
bullets: Built and deployed a multi-agent job-search platform (FastAPI on Render, Next.js on Vercel, Supabase Postgres) that scrapes 12 job boards, scores listings via a hybrid keyword+LLM pipeline, and auto-generates tailored PDF CVs per application
Designed a provider-agnostic AI abstraction supporting both a cloud LLM API and a local CLI-based model, with automatic fail-open fallback and rate-limit-aware model cascading
Implemented an AI mock-interview trainer with scored, topic-based feedback sessions

flagship.name: Outreach Automation — Freelance Client Acquisition System
flagship.description: Freelance client-acquisition automation: an Airtable-backed lead database drives 4 n8n workflows (daily outreach send, automated follow-up, inbound reply detection, Apollo.io lead import) surfaced through a Next.js analytics dashboard.
highlights: 4 built n8n workflows covering the full outreach loop: import leads → send → follow up → detect replies
Apollo.io integration for automated lead sourcing
Next.js dashboard with Recharts visualizations for outreach funnel metrics
bullets: Designed a freelance-lead-generation automation system combining Airtable, n8n workflow automation, and Apollo.io lead sourcing
Built a Next.js analytics dashboard visualizing outreach funnel performance (sent, followed-up, replied) via Recharts

flagship.name: IoT Weather Dashboard — Frontend
flagship.description: Live dashboard visualizing real-time sensor readings (temperature/humidity/weather data) streamed from a physical IoT device via the companion backend API.
highlights: Paired with a real Express/MongoDB backend ingesting live sensor data — a genuine hardware-adjacent project, distinct from the web-only portfolio work
bullets: Built a live IoT sensor dashboard in React consuming real-time weather/environmental data from a physical device's backend API

flagship.name: IoT Weather Dashboard — Backend API
flagship.description: REST API ingesting and serving sensor readings from a physical IoT weather device into MongoDB, consumed by the companion frontend dashboard.
highlights: Single-purpose Express 5 API with a `Reading` model — small but a genuine hardware-data-ingestion backend, not a CRUD demo
bullets: Built an Express/MongoDB REST API to ingest and serve real-time sensor readings from a physical IoT device

flagship.name: Inventory Manager — Lot-Based Stock Ledger
flagship.description: A batch/lot-based inventory management system (built for a produce/fruit-stock use case) with products, vendors, and customers CRUD, buy/sell transaction ledgers, weighted-average cost calculation across lots, and a soft-delete trash bin.
highlights: Lot-based inventory with weighted-average rate calculation across purchase batches
Business-logic validation: rejects sales recorded before a lot's purchase date to prevent ledger inconsistencies
Dashboard-summary and stock API routes for real-time inventory position
Soft-delete (trash) pattern instead of hard deletes on products/vendors/customers
bullets: Built a lot-based inventory management system (Next.js, MongoDB) with weighted-average cost tracking across purchase batches
Implemented transaction-safety validation logic (date-ordering checks) to prevent inconsistent stock ledger states
Designed products/vendors/customers CRUD with a soft-delete trash pattern instead of destructive deletes

flagship.name: Zing Bliss Events — Luxury Event Agency Platform
flagship.description: A high-end event-management agency platform going well beyond a marketing site: public routes (home, about, services, gallery, per-event case studies, contact) plus an interactive AI-style client-preference quiz, a private authenticated client portal with live event itineraries, and an admin dashboard/CMS for creating and managing events.
highlights: Atomic-design component architecture (atoms/molecules/organisms) for a large, motion-heavy Next.js 16 + Tailwind 4 codebase
GSAP + Lenis smooth-scroll cinematic animation system across every route
Client-facing 'style quiz' feature for lead qualification
Private client portal (event itineraries, live countdowns) distinct from the public marketing routes
Admin CMS route for creating/managing events with a live preview
bullets: Built a full event-management agency platform (Next.js 16, GSAP, Lenis) with public marketing routes, an interactive client-preference quiz, a private client portal, and an admin CMS for event management
Implemented a GSAP/Lenis-driven cinematic animation system across an atomic-design component architecture
Designed and shipped an authenticated client portal separate from the public site for live event itinerary tracking

flagship.name: Gym Tracker — Workout Analytics & Progressive-Overload Coach
flagship.description: A full-stack fitness tracker: workout logging, routine builder, a per-exercise analytics view (dynamic `[exercise]` route) charting historical performance via Recharts, and an algorithmic progressive-overload coach that suggests the next workout's weights/reps based on logged history. Auth via NextAuth with a MongoDB adapter (GitHub OAuth).
highlights: Multi-user auth (NextAuth + MongoDB adapter, GitHub OAuth)
Per-exercise analytics with historical trend charts (Recharts)
Algorithmic (non-LLM) progressive-overload suggestion logic based on logged workout history
Routines, workout history, and settings as first-class features, not just a logging form
bullets: Built a full-stack workout tracker (Next.js, MongoDB, NextAuth) with multi-user auth via GitHub OAuth
Designed an algorithmic progressive-overload coach that analyzes logged workout history to suggest next-session weights/reps
Implemented per-exercise historical analytics dashboards using Recharts

flagship.name: Stock Trading Demo App
flagship.description: A stock/trading demo application with authenticated and public route groups, background job processing via Inngest (for scheduled/async tasks like email notifications), and a Radix UI component system — the most feature-dense of the standalone apps (auth, background jobs, email, form validation).
highlights: Inngest-based background job processing — a genuinely production-grade pattern for async work (not just synchronous API routes)
better-auth for authentication instead of a hand-rolled solution
Radix UI + react-hook-form for accessible, validated forms
Nodemailer integration for transactional email
bullets: Built a stock-trading demo app (Next.js 15, MongoDB) with background job processing via Inngest for async/scheduled tasks
Implemented authentication with better-auth and accessible form UI with Radix UI + react-hook-form
Integrated transactional email (Nodemailer) triggered from background jobs

flagship.name: Sweet Shop Management System (TDD Kata)
flagship.description: A test-driven-development kata: a small sweet-shop inventory CRUD app (add/search/filter sweets by name, category, price) built against a mocked REST API (json-server), with component-level Jest + React Testing Library coverage on every major component.
highlights: Deliberate TDD exercise — tests written alongside components (Navbar, SweetCard, AddSweet)
Mocked REST backend via json-server rather than a real database, keeping the exercise focused on frontend logic + testing discipline
bullets: Completed a TDD kata building a sweet-shop inventory CRUD app (React, Vite) with full Jest + React Testing Library component coverage

flagship.name: Personal Portfolio Site
flagship.description: Personal developer portfolio site — the canonical entry point for the rest of this project list, kept pinned on GitHub.

flagship.name: DevEvents — Event Discovery Platform
flagship.description: A dynamic event discovery platform with server-side rendering and external REST API integration for real-time event data.
highlights: Already present in the base resume's project list — kept here for a unified data source
bullets: Built a dynamic event discovery platform (Next.js, TypeScript) with SSR and real-time external API integration

gallery.name: Landing Page & UI Clone Gallery (36 projects)
gallery.description: 36 pixel-perfect landing-page and UI clones spanning very different client verticals (travel, astrology, legal, dental, hospitality, SaaS, weddings, and more) — 32 of 36 deployed live on Vercel. Individually these read as template work, but collectively they demonstrate the ability to ship a working, polished, deployed site fast and repeatedly across unrelated design briefs.
bullets: Designed and deployed 36 pixel-perfect landing-page/UI clones across travel, legal, hospitality, astrology, and SaaS verticals — 32 live on Vercel
Demonstrates rapid, repeatable delivery: pixel-accurate implementation, GSAP/Framer Motion animation, and responsive layout across dozens of unrelated design systems
